qualifying_org_definition: Any employer, work program, or volunteer site that can provide written verification of hours; policy cites unpaid volunteer work and community service as explicitly qualifying. No restriction to 501(c)(3) stated in policy text — site must be able to sign a written statement or be contactable as a collateral contact.
verification_instrument: Written statement from volunteer site (letter); EVF-E form F-10146 (Employer Verification of Earnings) is one named option but any written statement from the volunteer site is accepted per policy section 3.17.1
hours_formula_detail: Policy section 3.17.1 states ABAWDs must work or volunteer a minimum of 80 hours per month. Workfare sub-track under FSET uses allotment/minimum-wage formula but applies only to FSET-placed workfare participants, not the general volunteer lane.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Policy section 3.17.1 imposes no geographic restriction on where the volunteer site is located. The verification requirement is simply a written statement from 'the employer, work program, or volunteer site' with no in-state or Wisconsin-registration requirement stated. Federal default (any qualifying organization) applies by silence.
geographic_waivers: None as of 2026. Wisconsin previously had ABAWD time limit waivers covering 8 counties, Racine City, and 10 reservation areas through September 30, 2025. All waivers were terminated following the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA, enacted July 2025), which eliminated the waiver authority. No active waivers as of June 2026.
submission_deadline: By the 10th of the month following the month in which hours dropped below 80 (per 3.17.1 change-reporting rule). Positive verification of qualifying hours is submitted to the county income maintenance agency typically as part of monthly case review.
notes:
Wisconsin FoodShare ABAWD policy (handbook 3.17.1, current through release 25-03) explicitly lists unpaid volunteer work and community service as qualifying work toward the 80-hour monthly requirement. FSET participation is stated to be voluntary — recipients are not required to enroll in FSET or obtain county assignment before independent volunteer hours count. Verification is a written statement from the volunteer site or a collateral contact call; the EVF-E (F-10146) is one named instrument but not the only acceptable form. County variation flag is set because Wisconsin uses county-level income maintenance agencies, which may differ in practice, but the state policy framework is clean with no mandatory prestep.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial check conducted to try to refute the Tier 1 / no-prestep classification. Four angles searched: (1) work registration as a hidden prestep, (2) FSET case manager requirement, (3) approved volunteer site list, (4) FSET State Plan FY2025 for gating disclosures. Findings: (1) Section 3.16.1 (Basic Work Rules) explicitly states that work registration requirements are 'different from the FoodShare work requirement for certain able-bodied adults without dependents (ABAWDs)' — work registration is a general eligibility rule and is NOT a prestep gating ABAWD volunteer hour counting under 3.17.1. (2) FSET workfare (via FSET Handbook section 1.4) does require a formal agency-employer site agreement and case manager assignment before a participant can be placed — but this is the FSET/workfare component track only. The general volunteer lane in 3.17.1 lists 'unpaid work (such as, volunteer work, community service)' as an independent qualifying category that does not flow through FSET. A Wisconsin Watch article (March 2026) describes FSET workfare as requiring a case manager, which could be misread as applying to all volunteer work — it does not; the article is describing the FSET workfare sub-track, not the independent 3.17.1 lane. (3) No pre-approved volunteer site list was found for the general lane. The FSET workfare track does maintain agency-established site agreements, but these apply only to FSET-placed workfare participants. (4) FSET State Plan PDF (ffy2025) returned HTTP 403 and could not be reviewed directly. The Wisconsin DHS guide P-03606 (02/2026) also returned 403. However, the 3.17.1 handbook page (fetchable) confirmed all three qualifying sub-elements: independent volunteer work is listed, no site pre-approval is required, and verification is a written statement from the volunteer site. Tier 1 classification is confirmed. Confidence held at high based on multi-source confirmation across the primary policy handbook (3.17.1), the structural separation language in 3.16.1, the FSET handbook (1.4) showing workfare as a distinct sub-track, and the 2026 Wisconsin Watch article which, when read against the policy text, corroborates the separation rather than contradicting it.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: 3.17.1 explicitly states '80 hours per month' — federal_80 applies to the general volunteer lane. The FSET workfare sub-track uses an allotment/minimum-wage formula but this is a separate track not applicable to independent volunteers. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: Policy is silent on geographic location of volunteer org — no in-state requirement stated anywhere in 3.17.1 or related policy. Federal default (any qualifying org) applies; marked 'silent'. (3) Geographic waivers: Wisconsin had waivers covering 8 counties, Racine City, and 10 reservation areas through Sept 30, 2025; all were terminated under OBBBA. No active waivers as of June 2026. (4) Submission UX: Written verification is submitted to county income maintenance agency (paper); change-reporting rule in 3.17.1 sets a 'by the 10th of the following month' deadline for reporting when hours fall below 80. Submission mechanism is paper to county office. (5) Form fields: EVF-E F-10146 (Columbia County version extracted from PDF) is an employer wage form — not specifically a volunteer hours form. Fields include employer name/address/phone/FEIN/signature/print name/title and employee name/hours-per-week. For general volunteer written statements, no specific field list is mandated; the standard baseline applies. Form_required_fields reflects the practical expectation based on F-10146 structure plus what a county IM agency would need to verify hours.
